title: centaurea pseudophrygia subsp. alutacea
intro: Centaurea pseudophrygia subsp. alutacea, also known as the alutacea knapweed, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the daisy family that is native to the Balkans and parts of Asia Minor. This plant has been found to possess medicinal properties, particularly in its ability to act as an anti-inflammatory and antioxidant agent. The health benefits associated with Centaurea pseudophrygia subsp. alutacea include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve digestion, and boost the immune system. Traditional herbal preparations of this plant include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties and promote overall well-being.
